description: Install or update BMad Builder module config and help entries.
args: {-H: headless mode}|{inline values: skip prompts with provided values}

description: Create, edit, or rebuild an agent skill through conversational discovery.
args: {-H: headless mode}|{description: initial agent concept}|{path: existing agent to edit or rebuild}

description: Run quality analysis on an existing agent — structure, cohesion, prompt craft, and enhancement opportunities.
Create, edit, or rebuild a workflow or utility skill.
args: {-H: headless mode}|{description: initial skill concept}|{path: existing skill to edit or rebuild}

description: Run quality analysis on an existing workflow/skill — structure, efficiency, and enhancement opportunities.
Convert any skill to BMad-compliant, outcome-driven equivalent with before/after HTML comparison report.
args: {--convert: path or URL to source skill}|{-H: headless mode}

description: Brainstorm and plan a BMad module — explore ideas, decide architecture, and produce a build plan.
Scaffold module infrastructure into built skills, making them an installable BMad module.
args: {-H: headless mode}|{path: skills folder or single SKILL.md}

description: Check that a module's structure is complete, accurate, and all capabilities are properly registered.
args: {-H: headless mode}|{path: module or skill to validate}

description: Use early in ideation or when stuck generating ideas.
Orchestrate multi-agent discussions when you need multiple perspectives or want agents to collaborate.
Use when LLM needs to understand available docs without loading everything.
Use when doc becomes too large (>500 lines) to manage effectively.
Use after drafting to polish written content.
outputs: three-column markdown table with suggested fixes

description: Use when doc produced from multiple subprocesses or needs structural improvement.
Use for quality assurance or before finalizing deliverables. Code Review in other modules runs this automatically, but also useful for document reviews.
Use alongside adversarial review for orthogonal coverage — method-driven not attitude-driven.

Use to distill any intent input (brief, PRD, transcript, brain dump, design folder, mixed multi-source) into a succinct, no-fluff SPEC.md contract + companions that downstream work derives from. Locks the WHAT before the HOW. Works for software, game design, research, editorial, policy, business, anything intent-bearing. Validation mode also available.

Use when you want to change how an agent or workflow behaves — add persistent facts, swap templates, insert activation hooks, or customize menus. Scans what's customizable, picks the right scope (agent vs workflow), writes the override to _bmad/custom/, and verifies the merge. No TOML hand-authoring required.

Use to pressure-test and harden an idea — software, business, creative, research, or life — until it proves out, hardens into something buildable, or dies cheaply. Persona-driven interrogation; optional handoff to bmad-spec or bmad-quick-dev.
Orient to the current project position and determine the next steps.
Generate the static Excalidraw diagram of the CPM Cinematics-as-Code methodology.
Generate and maintain the living Excalidraw diagram of the current production state.
Summarize production progress — scenes, shards, and what to do next — from the Slate.
Read project state and hand off to the right specialist agent or workflow.
Review a scene for thematic alignment and break it into atomic beats, producing Showrunner Notes.
outputs: Showrunner Notes with atomic beat breakdown

description: Extract and sharpen the pre-authored beat for the current shard, producing its Showrunner Notes.
Track narrative contracts through the Plant, Maintain, and Payoff lifecycle.
Answer story, character, and world questions from canonical Show Bible content.
outputs: Canonical answer grounded in the Show Bible

description: Architect the full visual specification for one beat — lighting, lens, color, and composition.
Answer a visual-language question with the canonical Style Guide rule and its rationale.
Scan a draft prompt for banned words and missing required terms.
Validate shard continuity, inject missing character state, define the handshake, and halt on failure.
outputs: Continuity status (PASS/INJECT/FAIL), injected state, and handshake definition

description: Verify the prior shard entry contract was honored by the current shard.
Update a character file with new mutable state and append a version-history row.
outputs: Updated character file with version-history row

description: Synthesize the Showrunner, Cinematographer, and Script Supervisor outputs into a final AI video prompt.
Audit an existing draft prompt for anchoring, vocabulary, exit hooks, and injections.
outputs: Compliance report (PASS or FAIL) with fixes

description: Compile a prompt for a 5s, 15s, or 30s shard with scaled choreography.
outputs: Scaled compiled prompt with Build Notes

description: Run a vision interview and scaffold a new project with draft Show Bible, Style Guide, and characters.
outputs: Project scaffold, draft Show Bible, draft Style Guide, draft characters, methodology diagram

description: Extend existing inception drafts with deeper conversation or add missed phases.
outputs: Updated draft artifacts and memlog entries

description: Grade foundation readiness — artifact presence, draft honesty, and structural validity.
outputs: Readiness verdict (READY or ON HOLD) with per-artifact report

description: Scaffold a new CPM production project — directory tree, config, manifest, slate, and methodology diagram.
args: {project_name: film or series name}|{model_target: e.g. sora/kling/runway/pika}|{shard_duration: 5/15/30}|{-location: custom path}
outputs: New project tree with .cpm/config.yaml, manifest, Slate, and methodology diagram

description: Repair missing scaffolding or reconfigure project settings without touching creative content.
outputs: Updated scaffolding files and created missing directories

description: Check that an existing project is structurally complete and production-ready.
outputs: Validation report (pass or hold) with blocking issues

description: Author a new Show Bible — logline, pillars, world rules, arcs, and motifs — via filmmaker dialogue.
Revise sections of an existing Show Bible, surfacing conflicts with prior decisions first.
Grade an existing Show Bible against the contract for completeness and substance.
outputs: Validation verdict (PASS, HOLD with gaps, or NO BIBLE)

description: Define a production visual language and write the four architecture files.
Revise or extend an existing style guide, surfacing conflicts and applying changes across files.
outputs: Updated architecture files and memlog entries

description: Grade a style guide for completeness, exact hex codes, and banned/required word lists.
outputs: Validation report (PASS or HOLD) with gaps

description: Create a new character with a full visual-identity interview capturing immutable LEFT/RIGHT features.
outputs: Character file, roster entry, and decision trail

description: Update an existing character with mutable state changes and append a version-history row.
outputs: Updated character file with version-history row

description: Grade a character file against the contract and confirm continuity-ready status.
Author a new scene brief with filmmaker-directed beats, setting, characters, and purpose.
outputs: scene-brief.md, entry contract, manifest registration, and Slate update

description: Revise an existing scene brief — setting, characters, beats, durations, or narrative.
outputs: Updated scene-brief.md, manifest and Slate updates

description: Grade a scene brief for structural completeness, beat specificity, and production readiness.
outputs: Validation report (PASS, PASS WITH WARNINGS, or HOLD)

description: Run the Four-Agent Ritual to generate one continuity-safe AI video prompt and exit state for a beat.
outputs: Shard prompt, exit state, and manifest and Slate updates

description: Re-run the ritual to overwrite an already-generated beat prompt and exit state.
outputs: Overwritten shard prompt and exit state; downstream shards invalidated

description: Grade an already-generated shard for continuity safety and structural completeness.
outputs: Validation report (PASS, PASS WITH WARNINGS, or HOLD)

description: Grade one shard boundary on five continuity criteria plus the MUST-NOT-Show floor.
args: {scene: zero-padded scene number}|{shard: shard A number}

description: Chain handshake tests across a scene for the three-consecutive-pass verdict.
outputs: Scene validation report (VALIDATED or NOT VALIDATED)
